slides.title: Case Overview
bullets: Diagnosis: Lumbar spinal stenosis
70-year-old male
Conservative treatment
Lives alone; needs subway and bus for hospital visits

slides.title: Chief Complaint and Goal
bullets: Chief complaint: unable to walk independently
Goal: independent outdoor ambulation with cane or walker
Current mobility: independent wheelchair mobility

slides.title: Evaluation Findings
bullets: Low back pain during bed mobility and transfers
Limited left hip flexion ROM
Left lower extremity strength at MMT 4 level
FBS: 24 points
Moderate superficial sensory dullness in left lower extremity

slides.title: Gait and Precautions
bullets: Left knee back knee during gait
Quadriceps-dominant compensation observed
Corset in use
Left lower extremity DVT

slides.title: Problem List
bullets: Reduced left lower extremity support
Reduced sensory input during standing and gait
Decreased balance with high fall risk
Inefficient and unsafe gait pattern
Insufficient outdoor mobility for independent living

slides.title: Clinical Interpretation
bullets: Pain, ROM restriction, weakness, sensory loss, and poor balance limit gait independence
Back knee likely appears as compensation for poor left-side support
Practical outdoor walking ability is essential due to living situation

slides.title: Current Treatment and Rationale
bullets: Barefoot standing balance practice: target standing control and sensory use
Ergometer: maintain activity level and endurance with lower walking load
Exercise therapy in lumbar spinal stenosis is associated with improved pain and function

slides.title: Treatment Plan and Goals
bullets: Prioritize safety with DVT, pain, and corset precautions
Improve left lower extremity support and standing balance
Reduce gait compensation and improve walking stability
Aim for independent outdoor ambulation with cane or walker
